Dexterity is a Buff that increases the Block gained directly from cards. This does not apply to any other sources of Block , including by Buffs gained from cards. Dexterity can be negative, reducing the Block gained from cards to a minimum of 0. Stacking: Intensity Caps: -999 to 999

Doom is a Debuff that the Necrobinder can apply to Enemies, or to herself. At the end of its turn, if an enemy with less HP than it has Doom , it will die. If the Necrobinder (or another character) ends her turn with less HP than Doom, she will die. Doom does not interact with the enemy's Block , and each Artifact stack will negate all Doom applied by a single source. Stacking: Intensity

Orbs are a mechanic used by The Defect . They operate identical to their Slay the Spire origins, with the addition of a new Orb type. Orbs have two abilities each, a Passive effect that happens every turn, and an Evoke effect. Using Orbs requires a player have Orb Slots . Each slot can hold one Orb. Orb Slots follow a first-in, first-out structure. Visually, Orbs are both Channeled and Evoked in right-to left order, with Orbs shifting right to fill any empty slots. The Defect will start combat with 3 Orb Slots. Gaining a Orb as any character without Orb Slots will add 1 Orb Slot. You can have a maximum of 10 Orb Slots at once. When an Orb is Channeled , it is placed into the right-most available Orb Slot. If all Orb Slots are occupied, the right-most Orb is Evoked, making space for the newly Channeled Orb. When an Orb is Evoked , it is removed from its Slot (allowing Orbs behind it to move forward), and its Evoke effect is activated. Each Orb type has a Passive ability which is activated automatically once per turn. Lightning , Frost , Dark , and Glass Orbs activate their Passives at end of turn, while Plasma Orbs activate theirs at the beginning of your turn. There are five typ...

Poison is a Debuff that the Silent can apply to Enemies. At the beginning of its turn, an enemy with Poison will lose HP equal to its number of Poison stacks. Then, its Poison stack will decrease by 1. Poison inflicts HP loss after the enemy's Block is removed, and each Artifact stack will negate all Poison applied by a single source. Stacking: Intensity

Strength is a Buff that increases the damage of Attack cards and enemy Attacks. Each point of Strength increases the damage of an attack by 1. Attacks that hit multiple times, like Sword Boomerang , will apply Strength to each individual hit. Strength can be negative, reducing the damage of attacks to a minimum of 0 damage per hit. Notes: Negative Strength is possible. Stacking: Intensity Caps: -999 to 999

Vulnerable is a Debuff both players and enemies can apply to their targets. While available to all characters, the Ironclad has the most dedicated support for Vulnerable . While a character or enemy has Vulnerable , the damage received from Attack cards and enemy Attacks is increased by 50% (typically). At the end of its turn, one point of Vulnerable is decremented from the player or enemy. Stacking: Duration
Gain X less per turn. Stacking: Intensity

Weak is a Debuff both players and enemies can apply to their targets. While available to all characters, the Silent has the some dedicated support for Weak . While a character or enemy has Weak , the damage of their Attack cards or enemy Attacks is decreased by 25% (typically), then rounded down. At the end of its turn, one point of Weak is decremented from the player or enemy. Stacking: Duration
At the end of your turn, Retain up to X cards. Stacking: Intensity
